description: Replace polling-based agent and status synchronization in frontend/game.js with a single SSE connection to /ui/events while preserving fetch-based fallback behavior.

userStories.title: Inventory existing sync entry points in game.js
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the current polling-based status and agent sync entry points mapped so that the SSE refactor only changes the required paths in frontend/game.js.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/game.js includes implementation notes or task comments identifying the current fetchStatus() and fetchAgents() callers
The ownership boundary for main-agent status updates, office-agent updates, and fallback activation is documented in the same file or adjacent task notes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Documented the current polling sync entry points directly in frontend/game.js, including the bootstrap callers in create(), the recurring callers in update(), the setState() one-shot fetchStatus() refresh, and the ownership boundary between main-agent status updates, office-agent rendering, and future SSE fallback gating.

userStories.title: Extract reusable main-agent apply helper
userStories.description: As a developer, I need main-agent status application moved into a reusable helper so that SSE state payloads and fallback fetches update the player state through one code path.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/game.js defines a helper that applies main-agent state and detail updates from plain payload data rather than a fetch response object
fetchStatus() delegates main-agent rendering or state mutation to the shared helper
The shared helper can be called during initial SSE state bootstrap without additional fetch-only assumptions
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Extract reusable office-agent lifecycle helpers
userStories.description: As a developer, I need office-agent sprite lifecycle logic split into shared helpers so that SSE events and fallback fetches manage remote agents consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/game.js defines shared helpers for creating, updating, removing, and marking office agents offline from agent payload data
fetchAgents() uses the shared office-agent helpers instead of duplicating sprite lifecycle logic inline
Helper inputs are based on agent payload objects and do not depend on fetch-specific control flow
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add SSE mode state and payload parsing helpers
userStories.description: As a developer, I need explicit SSE mode flags and defensive payload parsing helpers so that stream health and malformed event handling are centralized before wiring scene integration.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/game.js defines scene-level state for tracking whether SSE is active or degraded to polling fallback
frontend/game.js defines helper logic for safely parsing SSE event payloads and ignoring malformed JSON or missing required fields
Unknown or partial SSE payloads do not throw uncaught exceptions from the shared parsing path
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create EventSource lifecycle during scene setup
userStories.description: As a user, I need the scene to open one EventSource connection during creation so that live office updates begin immediately and can be cleaned up safely.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/game.js opens an EventSource to /ui/events during scene creation
The EventSource instance is stored on the scene or an equivalent lifecycle-owned object so it is initialized once per scene lifecycle
Open and error handlers are registered on the EventSource for healthy-stream and degraded-mode transitions
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Bootstrap scene state from SSE state event
userStories.description: As a user, I need the initial office state to load from the SSE state event so that the main agent and visible office agents render without waiting for a polling round trip.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/game.js listens for the SSE state event
The state event payload initializes main-agent status and detail via the shared main-agent helper
The state event payload initializes the visible office-agent collection via the shared office-agent helpers
Scene bootstrap can render current server state before normal polling runs
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Handle incremental agent SSE events
userStories.description: As a user, I need incremental SSE events applied immediately so that agent joins, updates, departures, and offline transitions are reflected in the office without delay.
acceptanceCriteria: agent_join events create a remote agent immediately through the shared office-agent helper
agent_update events update an existing remote agent's rendered location, detail, or status presentation through the shared helper path
agent_leave events remove the matching remote agent from scene state and rendering
agent_offline events mark the matching remote agent as offline without removing unrelated agents
Unknown event types or partial agent payloads are ignored safely without stopping later event handling
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Gate polling while SSE is healthy
userStories.description: As a developer, I need existing polling loops guarded by SSE health so that fetchStatus() and fetchAgents() do not duplicate live updates while the stream is working.
acceptanceCriteria: Normal fetchStatus() polling is skipped while the scene is in healthy SSE mode
Normal fetchAgents() polling is skipped while the scene is in healthy SSE mode
Existing polling timers or guards are updated so the same agent state is not applied twice during healthy SSE operation
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Restore polling fallback and scene cleanup around SSE failure
userStories.description: As a user, I need fetch-based synchronization to resume after SSE errors and clean up correctly on teardown so that the office stays current when the stream fails or the scene is destroyed.
acceptanceCriteria: SSE error handling enables fetchStatus() and fetchAgents() fallback polling after stream failure
Recovery logic can disable fallback polling again if SSE reconnects or a new healthy EventSource is established
Scene teardown closes the active EventSource and clears any related SSE mode flags or listeners
Typecheck passes
